nodes.label: Truncated SVD for Online RSS-to-Distance Mapping Without Site Survey
nodes.source_file: raw/Zero-Configuration, Robust Indoor Localization Theory and Experimentation.pdf
nodes.rationale: Using truncated SVD on online AP-to-AP RSS measurements creates a real-time calibration of the RF environment without site surveys, capturing dynamic effects (door states, furniture, people) and bootstrapping zero-configuration localization.
nodes.norm_label: truncated svd for online rss-to-distance mapping without site survey

nodes.label: Offline/Online Phases of RSS Fingerprinting-Based Localization
nodes.source_file: raw/Sci-Hub. Optimal placement of reference nodes for wireless indoor positioning systems : 2014 11th In.pdf
nodes.rationale: The two-phase structure (offline survey + online matching) is universal to fingerprinting systems; offline builds a radio map by RSS site survey, online matches live RSS to the map. This design separates calibration cost from operational cost.
nodes.norm_label: offline/online phases of rss fingerprinting-based localization

nodes.label: Low-Rank Matrix Completion for Radio Map Construction
nodes.source_file: raw/Wang et al. - 2021 - Fast Construction of the Radio Map Based on the Im.pdf | My Library | Zotero.pdf
nodes.rationale: RSS data exhibits spatial correlation and low-rank structure; exploiting this via matrix completion fills a sparse set of measured reference points into a complete radio map, reducing manual data collection cost.
nodes.norm_label: low-rank matrix completion for radio map construction

nodes.label: Distribution of WLAN RSSI for Indoor Location Determination
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.norm_label: distribution of wlan rssi for indoor location determination

nodes.label: RSSI Distribution Properties (Left-Skew, Lognormal)
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.rationale: Understanding the actual statistical distribution of RSSI (left-skewed at strong signal, approximately lognormal at weak signal) is key to improving indoor fingerprinting positioning performance and building better radio maps instead of storing entire histograms.
nodes.norm_label: rssi distribution properties (left-skew, lognormal)

nodes.label: WLAN Card Hardware Variation in RSSI Reporting
nodes.author: Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.rationale: Different WLAN chipsets implement RF measurement differently (range, granularity, mapping), requiring consistent card use during both offline and online phases of fingerprinting to avoid cross-card RSSI mismatch errors.
nodes.norm_label: wlan card hardware variation in rssi reporting

nodes.label: Nearest Neighbors in Signal Space (NNSS) Algorithm
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.rationale: NNSS computes Euclidean distance in signal space between observed SS measurements and recorded reference SS values, picking the closest-matching location. It avoids the need for complex geometric triangulation by operating directly in signal space.
nodes.norm_label: nearest neighbors in signal space (nnss) algorithm

nodes.label: Wall Attenuation Factor (WAF) Propagation Model
nodes.author: Paramvir Bahl and Venkata N. Padmanabhan
nodes.rationale: WAF was chosen over Rayleigh and Rician models as a compromise between simplicity and accuracy; it accounts for large-scale path loss and wall obstructions without requiring complex per-component isolation, and enables cost-effective deployment without dense empirical measurements.
nodes.norm_label: wall attenuation factor (waf) propagation model

nodes.label: Sum-RSS Floor Estimation Algorithm
nodes.author: Kriangkrai Maneerat, Chutima Prommak, Kamol Kaemarungsi
nodes.rationale: Summing average RSS from all reference nodes on each floor avoids time-consuming offline radio map construction; the floor with the highest RSS sum is selected. This low-complexity approach is suited for resource-constrained WSN nodes while achieving >90% floor accuracy.
nodes.norm_label: sum-rss floor estimation algorithm

nodes.label: Weighted Average Filter with Threshold (RSSI Filtering)
nodes.author: Juthatip Wisanmongkol et al.
nodes.rationale: A low-complexity filter that excludes spike outliers using a counter-threshold mechanism, updating the weighted average only when persistent change is detected. This preserves tracking ability while rejecting transient multipath spikes.
nodes.norm_label: weighted average filter with threshold (rssi filtering)

nodes.label: Maximum Ratio Combining (MRC) for BLE RSSI
nodes.author: Juthatip Wisanmongkol et al.
nodes.rationale: MRC weights contributions from multiple BLE tags proportionally to their signal strength relative to the minimum receivable level, providing more stable combined RSSI than simple mean or max combiners for space diversity.
nodes.norm_label: maximum ratio combining (mrc) for ble rssi
